title: Rolled beeswax advent candles
subtitle: Four tapers in deep red for the four weeks of Advent
excerpt: Four rolled beeswax taper candles made from honeycomb-sheet beeswax in deep red, one lit for each week of Advent. No melting required. The rolling technique works with children. Each taper burns approximately 2 hours per centimetre of width.
sourceNotes: Bramble Berry beeswax sheet rolling guides (brambleberry.com, accessed May 2026): wick sizing and rolling technique. Standard Advent tradition: four candles for four Sundays (three purple and one pink in the liturgical tradition; four red in the home tradition).

content.text: Rolled beeswax tapers are the easiest candle to make: no melting, no thermometer, no pour. A flat sheet of natural honeycomb-textured beeswax is warmed briefly, wrapped around a wick, and rolled into a tight cylinder. The result is a taper that burns with a clean, honey-warm scent and lasts 2-3 hours. In the Advent tradition, one candle is lit on the Sunday of each of the four weeks before Christmas.

of beeswax sheet is around 62°C; at room temperature (18-22°C) it is pliable but not sticky. In a room below 16°C, the sheet becomes brittle and cracks when rolled. Warm it for 10-15 seconds with a hairdryer on a low setting before rolling.

items.name: Beeswax pellets
items.prepNote: 4 x full sheets of dyed beeswax honeycomb (approximately 20 cm x 40 cm each, in deep red). Beeswax sheets sold for candle-rolling are a different product from pellets; they come pre-formed with the honeycomb texture. Cut each sheet in half lengthways if thinner tapers are preferred.

items.name: Candle wick, pre-tabbed (CD-12)
items.prepNote: Flat-braided cotton wick, 2/0 or 3/0 weight for a 3 cm diameter taper. Cut each piece 2 cm longer than the sheet width so there is a wick tab at the top. Pre-tabbed wicks work; square-braided flat wicks rolled in are also traditional.

content.text: If the room is below 18°C, pass a hairdryer on its lowest setting over each sheet for 10-15 seconds, at a distance of 15-20 cm. The sheet should flex without cracking.

Lay the sheet flat, honeycomb side up. Place a wick along one of the short edges, leaving 2 cm extending beyond the top (this becomes the lit end). Press the wick gently into the wax at the edge to hold it in place.

Starting at the wick edge, roll the sheet firmly and evenly, pressing down with both hands as you roll. Keep the roll tight and the edges aligned. A loose roll produces an uneven burn. At the end of the sheet, press the final edge firmly into the candle body to seal.
Make all four tapers. Stand them upright in a candle holder or lay flat on a tray. They are ready to burn immediately.

Store unused tapers flat in a cool, dark place, wrapped in tissue paper to prevent the honeycomb surface from sticking together. Beeswax is effectively indefinite in storage; the natural honey scent fades very slowly over years. A white surface bloom is normal wax migration, not spoilage.

recipe.yieldDescription: 4 taper candles (~20 cm x 3 cm, ~2 hours burn per candle)
